name: Bank Migration During Restructuring
description: A trusted vendor submits a routine-looking invoice during a period of corporate restructuring and finance team transition. The payment request appears legitimate in isolation, but cross-referencing internal history, policy thresholds, contact patterns, and external records shows the bank change was not validated through the required channel and the sender identity does not line up cleanly with the vendor's documented transition timeline.
why_hard: The invoice amount is within historical range, the vendor is known, the email thread appears continuous, and the cover story explains the banking change as part of a restructuring. A solo model reviewing only the current invoice and latest email would likely see a familiar vendor, plausible amount, and coherent explanation and conclude the payment should be allowed.
why_detectable: The fraud becomes visible only when multiple benign-looking facts are combined: the requested remittance account differs from the vendor master record, the sender was never previously used for payment instruction changes, the vendor's documented contacts do not match the person asserting authority, the policy requires out-of-band verification for any bank change regardless of amount, and external records show the claimed finance transition details are inconsistent with the timeline presented in the email.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Thanks again. Attaching the January invoice for the blister line run tied to PO 44118. Same scope as approved in December, with the overage from the secondary labeling pass included. Let me know if your team needs the batch summary resent.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Received, thank you. We have it queued for the next Wednesday wire cycle.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Jumping in from the parent entity side. Northbridge Clinical Packaging is consolidating treasury operations following the Q1 legal reorganization, so remittance for open invoices is now handled through the shared finance desk. Please update the banking details for INV-78431 and any February payments going forward. Lena is still managing operations, but cash application has moved to me during the transition. Revised remittance advice attached.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Thanks Daniel. We will update against this invoice.

email_chain.subject: Re: January packaging run / PO 44118
email_chain.body: Appreciated. For matching purposes, please keep the invoice number unchanged. The legal entity on your PO remains Northbridge Clinical Packaging LLC until the vendor master is refreshed, but receipts are being centralized during the transition.

context.org_policies: Changes to vendor master data, including payment instructions, require validation from a designated contact listed in the vendor record. Validation must occur via a pre-approved, out-of-band channel (e.g., phone call to a known number). AP is not authorized to alter master payment data based on email requests alone, regardless of the amount.

entity_registrations.filing_note: Internal merger and treasury centralization filing for selected subsidiaries; operating entities remain separately payable pending novation or customer notice.

required_evidence: The requested remittance account and routing number do not match the approved vendor master payment instructions.
The bank-change request originated from a first-seen sender ('daniel.reeves@northbridge-holdings.com') who is not a designated contact in the vendor master record.
Org policy prohibits AP from altering master payment data based on an email request and requires out-of-band validation with a known contact, which has not occurred.

The parent-entity restructuring story is contradicted by the external filing note, which states that operating entities like Northbridge Clinical Packaging LLC 'remain separately payable pending novation or customer notice'.
scoring_targets.predicted_solo_model_failure: A solo model reviewing only the latest invoice package would likely focus on the familiar vendor name, historically normal amount, plausible restructuring explanation, and the absence of obvious red flags like spoofing or urgency. Without synthesizing the procedural violation in the policy, the identity mismatch in the vendor record, and the contextual contradiction in the external filing note, it would treat the remittance update as a routine treasury migration and allow payment.
